title: Unit Fixtures, Properties, Fuzzing, Race Detection, and Leak Evidence · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in layered deterministic Go evidence suite and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Create small original RSS, Atom, namespace, encoding, date, identity, update, malformed, entity, oversized, and extension fixtures with explicit expected evidence.
Test URL, discovery, parse, normalize, schedule, state, cursor, search, and policy functions with injected clocks and deterministic inputs.
Fuzz XML tokens, URLs, dates, cursors, state transitions, and normalization with bounded invariants and preserved minimal failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 9929 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile original fixture coverage, pure units, fuzz properties, race and leak cases, and evidence non-claims; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Label browser models, unit fixtures, loopback HTTP, temporary PostgreSQL, race, fuzz, load, accessibility, platform, and production evidence separately.
